rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical multi-component hydroxy-thiocarbamation of electron-deficient alkenes with KSCN and H2O to give β-hydroxy primary S-thiocarbamates
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of SCN- to thiocyanyl radical (SCN•); the benzylic radical adduct is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: Benzylic carbon radical from SCN• addition to α-phenylacrylate, intermediate II
HFIP H-bond-activated thiocyanate adduct, intermediate V

electron_flow: SCN- is oxidized at the anode to SCN•, which adds to the electron-deficient alkene to give a benzylic C-radical; a second anodic SET produces a benzylic cation that is trapped by H2O to give a β-hydroxy thiocyanate; HFIP-assisted hydration of the SCN group then yields the primary S-thiocarbamate while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Target product 4a suppressed to trace; HRMS detected BHT-SCN adduct and BHT-carbon radical adduct, confirming SCN• (I) and benzylic radical (II)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: D-labeled 4a detected by HRMS; N-H/O-H of carbamoyl and hydroxyl originate from water

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O-labeled 4a detected by HRMS; both hydroxyl and carbamoyl oxygens come from H2O

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and KSCN
probe_reagents.observation: KSCN onset oxidation +0.383 V vs SCE, much lower than α-phenylacrylate (+1.693 V), indicating SCN- is preferentially oxidized at the anode

probe_reagents.name: Pd/C hydrogenation of N-phenylmaleimide using cell-evolved H2
probe_reagents.observation: N-phenylsuccinimide 6a obtained in 59% yield, confirming cathodic H2 evolution

notes: HFIP (solvent) is proposed to activate the SCN group via H-bonding (intermediate V), enabling nucleophilic hydration of the nitrile-like SCN to deliver the primary S-thiocarbamate; no DFT or KIE reported.
